ip_outcome_note: No suspected_ip flagged. Item card with original TCG art, no character IP.

trivia_bullets.text: Weakness Policy is an Uncommon Pokemon Tool in Burning Shadows (SM3, August 5, 2017). The set headlines Tapu Bulu-GX, Ho-Oh-GX, and Darkrai-GX. Weakness Policy is a utilitarian tool print within a GX-heavy fire/shadow-themed set.

Weakness Policy first appeared in English in Primal Clash (XY5, 2015, no. 142/160), making Burning Shadows its first reprint. The card originated in the Japanese Gaia Volcano subset (2014). Effect — 'the Pokemon this card is attached to has no Weakness' — has remained identical across all prints. No Weakness eliminates the TCG's standard 2x damage multiplier, a significant defensive swing.
sources: WebSearch: Bulbapedia Weakness Policy Primal Clash 142

trivia_bullets.text: In the SM Standard format (2017-2019), Weakness Policy saw niche use in decks running Pokemon with punishing Weaknesses — most notably Gardevoir-GX (Psychic Weakness to Metal) and Buzzwole-GX (Fighting Weakness to Psychic). Community consensus treated it as a tech option rather than a core slot; Choice Band's damage bonus was usually more universally valuable, and the Tool slot opportunity cost was high.
sources: WebSearch: Pokebeach forums Weakness Policy competitive

trivia_bullets.text: Art credited to Ayaka Yoshida, a Pokemon TCG illustrator active across the Sun and Moon era, credited on multiple Item and Trainer cards.

related_cards.name: Weakness Policy
related_cards.note: Original English print; same effect, XY-era art frame

related_cards.name: Choice Band
related_cards.note: The competing Tool slot in the same set; offensive counterpart to Weakness Policy's defensive effect

related_cards.name: Gardevoir-GX
related_cards.note: Same-set headline GX with Psychic Weakness to Metal; natural Weakness Policy candidate
